text: The Python programming language was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. It emphasizes code readability and simplicity, making it popular for beginners and experts alike.
A database index works like a book's table of contents. Instead of scanning every row, the database jumps directly to the location of the data, making queries dramatically faster.
Git stores snapshots of your project, not differences between files. Every commit is a complete picture of all files at that moment, linked to the previous commit.
The TCP protocol ensures reliable data delivery by breaking information into packets, sending them across the network, and reassembling them in the correct order at the destination.
Machine learning models learn patterns from training data. The more diverse and representative the data, the better the model generalizes to new, unseen examples.
In object-oriented programming, encapsulation means bundling data and the methods that operate on that data into a single unit, hiding internal details from the outside world.
The binary search algorithm finds an element in a sorted list by repeatedly dividing the search range in half, achieving logarithmic time complexity.
Docker containers package an application with all its dependencies, ensuring it runs identically on any machine regardless of the underlying operating system.
